Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. To calculate how many bottles she needs for a leap year, we first determine the number of days in a leap year, which is 366 days. Since she uses shampoo every other day, she will use it 366 / 2 = 183 times. Each bottle contains 14 ounces of shampoo, so the total amount of shampoo needed is 183 * 1/4 = 45.75 ounces. Since she can't buy a fraction of a bottle, she needs to round up to the next whole number, which is 46 ounces. This means she needs 46 / 14 = 3.2857 bottles. Therefore, she needs 4 bottles to get through a leap year.

[amount of shampoo used per day] is 1/4 ounce, and [number of days in a leap year] is 366. She uses [amount of shampoo used per day] every other day, so the total number of uses is [number of days in a leap year] / 2. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so the total amount of shampoo needed is [number of uses] * [amount of shampoo used per day]. She needs to round up to the next whole number of bottles, which is [number of bottles needed].
